marketState: neutral — bearish near-term weather offset by extreme 8-16D heat forecast and historically crowded short positioning
summary: NG=F is trading at $3.159, roughly 6.8% above its current fair-value midpoint of $2.958 but comfortably inside the model band ($2.458–$3.458), so the price is stretched but not extreme. Near-term demand (1-7D) is running 14% below the 5-year average and sits in the lower quartile of the historical range — a near-term bearish drag — but the 8-16 day demand outlook flips sharply, printing 41.3% above the 5-year normal and above the 5-year maximum, signaling an incoming extreme heat event. Managed money net positioning sits at just the 17th percentile of the 5-year range (net short -84,909 contracts), meaning the market is crowded short and vulnerable to a sharp squeeze if the heat materialises or extends. Storage at +153 Bcf above the 5-year average with a recent bearish injection surprise (+25 Bcf) is the primary structural headwind, limiting upside unless summer heat drives faster-than-expected draws. The seasonal signal is low-reliability (44 pp 10-90 spread) and mildly bullish only for weeks 1-3, with the typical 4-week move -2.3%; taken together the data favours waiting for a near-term weather dip to provide better entry toward fair value before positioning for the incoming heat catalyst.

longTerm.rationale: COT net at the 17th 5-year percentile (-84,909 contracts short) is a meaningful contrarian bullish setup, and the 8-16D demand surge above the 5-year max could establish a summer-heat narrative that burns into the storage surplus. However, storage is +153 Bcf above the 5-year average with a recent bearish surprise, and the seasonality signal has a 44 pp reliability spread — too noisy to commit size at the current slightly-above-fair-value price of $3.159. The winter strip (Dec '26 $3.977, Jan '27 $4.38) confirms structural demand is priced in further out, but the near term needs a cleaner entry.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surplus widens above +200 Bcf vs 5-year average; 8-16D heat forecast cools materially on model revisions; price breaks below $2.85 (outside the lower fair-value band) with no COT short-covering.
longTerm.trigger: Pullback to the $3.00–$3.05 zone (near the fair-value midpoint of $2.958–$2.968) AND at least one weekly storage report showing a draw or a build materially below the 5-year seasonal injection pace, confirming the heat wave is burning supply.

shortTerm.rationale: The 8-16D demand is above the 5-year maximum (+41.3% vs 5y, aboveMax=true) — a rare extreme that gas markets typically price in 3-5 days ahead as forecasters publish — while the 1h RSI has retreated to 36.1 (near oversold) and COT short crowding (17th pct) raises short-squeeze risk on any bullish headline. Near-term weather softness (1-7D at -14% vs 5y) is the entry opportunity: a dip toward $3.07-$3.10 pulls price back inside the fair-value midpoint zone and offers a defined stop below $2.95. TP1 $3.28 targets the upper mid-band; TP2 $3.45 approaches the current fair-value band ceiling of $3.458.
shortTerm.invalidation: Weather model consensus shifts 8-16D demand back toward or below 5-year normal; price closes below $2.95 on the daily bar; storage injection for the following week again surprises to the bearish side by more than 15 Bcf.

keyDrivers: 8-16D demand forecast above 5-year MAX (+41.3% vs 5y, aboveMax=true) — primary near-term bullish catalyst
COT managed-money net at 17th 5-year percentile (-84,909 contracts) — crowded short, short-squeeze vulnerability
Storage +153 Bcf above 5-year average with recent +25 Bcf bearish injection surprise — structural ceiling on rallies
Near-term (1-7D) demand 14% below 5-year average — short-term headwind creating potential entry opportunity on dip
Winter strip (Jan '27 $4.38 vs front $3.16) confirms market prices in tighter supply-demand into heating season
risks: Heat wave fails to materialise or forecast rolls back — removes the primary bullish catalyst and leaves COT shorts in control with storage as a further drag
Storage surplus accelerates above +200 Bcf vs 5-year average — would signal supply overwhelm even with summer heat demand
